done: Go project scaffolding (go.mod, go.sum, .gitignore, Makefile, README, LICENSE)
Internal documentation layout (PRD, package docs, spec docs, progress tracking)
Public foundation packages: geom, scene, input, physics, whisky
Project config format via whisky.json
CLI commands: whisky new, whisky run, whisky doctor
Embedded starter-project templates and local replace-aware scaffolding
Starter example project: pixel-quest
Unit test coverage for geom, input, scene, physics, and project scaffolding
Configurable keyboard mapping via Config.KeyMap (whisky.KeyMap) with semantic control names (move_up, move_down, move_left, move_right, action, sprint, confirm)
Two-pass UpdateInput for shared controls (reset all → OR pressed) preventing non-deterministic input
Main-thread runtime contract in whisky.Run
Window creation and teardown lifecycle
Event polling for window close and Escape key
Debug overlay rendering with SDL renderer text
Headless escape hatch for automation via WHISKY_HEADLESS
Tilemap package with TileSet, TileLayer, TileMap, and coordinate conversion
Tilemap builder API for programmatic level construction
Greedy merge collider generation from solid tiles
TileMapComponent scene integration with physics world
Tilemap JSON serialization and deserialization
Tilemap rendering interface stubs and viewport culling
Physics World.Clear and World.RemoveByPrefix for dynamic collider management
Pixel-quest example updated with tilemap-based level
Reserved assets directory conventions in generated projects
Render package with FillRect draw command and Camera2D (position, viewport, world-to-screen)
Closed DrawCmd pipeline with FillRect and SpriteCmd preserving insertion order
PNG texture loading contract through Context.LoadTexture, ready to be reattached to the native renderer stack
Spritesheet support in render with frame indexing and texture handles
Virtual resolution scaling policy exposed at the runtime/config layer
Primitive 2D rectangle rendering contract through the render draw-command pipeline
Camera2D integration in Context with automatic world-to-screen transform
Context.DrawRect API for queuing colored rectangles in world coordinates
Context.DrawSprite and Context.LoadTexture APIs for sprite rendering and texture loading
Scene drawable pass with Drawable components and SpriteComponent
TileMapComponent sprite rendering through render.Spritesheet
Native keyboard polling fed into input.State via UpdateInput
WASD and arrow key input bindings for player movement in pixel-quest example
Axis-separated collision response (resolveX / resolveY) against solid tile colliders
Pixel-quest example now renders tilemap and player with generated PNG sprites plus fallback rectangle drawing
physics.Collider.OneWay field for pass-through platforms; set automatically by GenerateColliders
Config.GravityY: configurable downward acceleration (px/s²), default 0 (backwards-compatible)
Platformer physics in pixel-quest: gravity accumulation, jump impulse, double jump (jumpsLeft), resolveX/resolveY with one-way platform awareness
Unit and integration tests for gravity, resolveY (solid/one-way/ceiling), and full tilemap→physics→game pipeline
Gameplay package with reusable Health component, overlap-based damage resolution, and PatrolComponent AI
Pixel-quest example upgraded with patrolling enemies, player attack hitbox, HP tracking, and contact damage
TargetComponent for sight-based chase behavior and DropComponent for reusable enemy rewards
Runtime backend abstraction via internal/platform interfaces (Platform, Renderer, Backend), decoupling whisky.Run from any concrete renderer implementation
Native window handle contract in internal/platform for future Vulkan, D3D12, X11, and Wayland surface creation
Initial Win32 native platform backend shell with window creation, event pumping, keyboard polling, resize tracking, and raw HWND/HINSTANCE export
Cross-platform-safe Win32 backend scaffolding via build tags plus Linux test pass and Windows cross-compilation validation
Initial X11 native platform backend with Xlib window creation through purego, event pumping, resize tracking, key name to keysym/keycode resolution, and raw Display*/Window export
Wayland native platform backend with libwayland-client + xdg-shell window creation, event pumping, close/resize handling, minimal keyboard integration, and raw wl_display/wl_surface export
Separate native-window desktop factory with OS-specific selection logic, including Linux X11/Wayland preference handling for future RHI-backed renderers
Initial RHI contract in internal/gfx/rhi covering Instance, Surface, Device, Swapchain, SurfaceTarget validation, and SwapchainDescriptor normalization
Vulkan dynamic loader on Linux and Windows with typed function binding for instance-level API entrypoints
Vulkan instance creation through the RHI layer with extension/layer availability checks, typed create-info structs, and deterministic unit tests
Vulkan instance extension and validation-layer selection based on NativeWindow handles (Win32, X11, Wayland)
Vulkan surface creation and destruction for Win32, X11, and Wayland through the RHI surface contract
SDL3 renderer path removed from whisky.Run and desktop backend selection; desktop rendering now hard-targets the Vulkan track
Vulkan physical-device discovery, queue-family selection, logical device creation, and swapchain creation through the RHI device/swapchain contracts
Desktop runtime backend now boots through native windows plus Vulkan instance/surface/device/swapchain instead of failing early
Vulkan 2D renderer bootstrap with swapchain image enumeration, image views, render pass, framebuffers, command pool/buffers, semaphores, fences, and graphics pipeline setup
First presented Vulkan frame path: acquire image, clear color attachment, submit, and present through the native swapchain
Runtime draw-command consumption on Vulkan for FillRect and SpriteCmd using a textured quad pipeline, dynamic vertex uploads, per-texture descriptors, and PNG texture upload to GPU memory
Virtual-resolution viewport mapping moved into the Vulkan renderer path, including centered letterboxing and pixel-perfect scaling policy
GPU debug/text overlay path using a bitmap font atlas generated at renderer startup and consumed through the same Vulkan quad pipeline
Lower-overhead 2D batching by merging contiguous draw runs that share the same texture while preserving draw order

Generic RHI contract covering ShaderModule, Pipeline, Buffer, Texture, DescriptorSet, CommandBuffer, Queue with descriptor types (BufferUsage, TextureUsage, VertexLayout, BlendState, PipelineDescriptor, CompareOp, PrimitiveTopology)
Vulkan backend refactored to implement the generic RHI GPU contract (ShaderModule, Buffer, Queue fully functional; Pipeline/Texture stubbed with validation)

Metal backend RHI fully implemented: Instance (MTLCreateSystemDefaultDevice), Surface (CAMetalLayer creation + view attachment), Device (swapchain/shader/pipeline/buffer/texture/queue creation), Swapchain (drawable size management), ShaderModule (MSL compilation), Pipeline (render pipeline state), Buffer/Texture/Queue/CommandBuffer (real Objective-C wrappers)
Audio engine with WAV and OGG Vorbis decoders, mixer with volume/pan/loop/pitch, and cross-platform output via oto/v3
Mouse and gamepad input API (MouseState, GamepadState with 15 buttons and 6 axes, Xbox-style naming) in the public input package
Mouse capture wired into all native backends: Win32 (WM_MOUSE*), X11 (ButtonPress/MotionNotify), Wayland (wl_pointer), macOS (NSEvent mouseDown/mouseMoved/scrollWheel)
XInput gamepad polling on Win32 with deadzone normalization, thumbstick and trigger mapping to GamepadState
input.State owns MouseState and [MaxGamepads]GamepadState with automatic NextFrame propagation
whisky.Context.Mouse() and Context.Gamepad(index) accessors for game code
Pixel-quest example supports mouse left-click for attack in addition to J/K keyboard
Asset cache and hot-reload API in the public assets package: thread-safe LRU cache, generic Get[T] loader, and recursive fsnotify watcher with invalidation callbacks
Assets cache and hot-reload fully wired into whisky.Run: Config.AssetsRoot/HotReload/CacheMaxSize, Context.Assets, fsnotify watcher with main-thread reload queue
Renderer LoadTexture supports in-place GPU texture replacement preserving TextureID for hot-reload (Vulkan + Metal)
Linux evdev gamepad polling via /dev/input/event* with button/axis mapping, deadzone normalization, and hotplug support (shared by X11 and Wayland backends)
macOS GameController.framework gamepad polling via purego/objc with GCExtendedGamepad button and axis mapping
Pixel-quest example uses relative asset paths via AssetsRoot and demonstrates hot-reload and mouse attack
Wayland xkbcommon integration for international keyboard layouts via purego dlopen, replacing hardcoded evdev keycodes with keysym-based mappings
Vulkan draw sorting by texture pointer using stable sort in buildDrawData, minimizing descriptor set rebinding and batch fragmentation
Persistent vertex buffer mapping: map once at buffer creation, eliminate per-frame vkMapMemory/vkUnmapMemory calls
Descriptor set leak fix: add VK_DESCRIPTOR_POOL_CREATE_FREE_DESCRIPTOR_SET_BIT to descriptor pool and free descriptor sets in gpuTexture.destroy, preventing crashes after ~1024 textures
Shared software renderer for 2D backend-agnostic vertex generation, texture batching, debug bitmap-font overlay, and virtual-resolution viewport mapping
Desktop Metal backend on macOS: native NSWindow via AppKit + Metal RHI instance/surface/device/swapchain integrated into whisky.Run
Metal 2D renderer with MSL vertex/fragment quad shader, dynamic MTLBuffer uploads, nearest sampler, and CAMetalLayer presentation loop
Pixel-quest example upgraded with full game-state machine: title screen, options, key-binding configuration, pause menu, game-over and victory screens
In-game UI toolkit in pixel-quest using whisky.Context.DrawRect: buttons, sliders, panels, and keyboard/mouse navigable menus
Interactive key rebinding in pixel-quest: select an action and press any key to remap; config persisted to assets/config.json
Pause menu overlay during gameplay with resume, options, and quit-to-title choices
Game-over and victory triggers with dedicated end-game screens and retry/play-again options
HUD health bar for player and enemies rendered via draw-command rectangles
Particle system for visual feedback: jump dust, landing puff, attack hit sparks, enemy death explosion, player hit blood, collectible sparkles, and projectile trails
Score/XP progression system with level thresholds, kill counter, coin currency, and XP-to-next leveling bar
Physical drop system: defeated enemies spawn floating pickups (xp, health, coin, item) with bob animation, despawn timer, and automatic collection on player overlap
Three enemy archetypes: patrolling slime, jumping frog, ranged shooter, and multi-shot boss with distinct colors and behaviors
Projectile system for shooter and boss enemies with collision against player and particle feedback
Three-level campaign (Tutorial, Caverns, Fortress) with unique tilemap layouts and escalating enemy variety
Level select menu with lock/unlock progression based on save data
Three-slot save system with Continue/New Game/Load Game flow; slots persist level, HP, score, key bindings, volume, and difficulty
Auto-save on level completion and manual save slot selection
remarks: Long-term engine direction is now explicit in code as well as docs: no SDL3 renderer fallback remains in the runtime. Desktop targets are native windows plus Vulkan on Linux/Windows and native NSWindow plus Metal on macOS.
next: Phase 5: add a Direct3D 12 backend on Windows after the RHI contract is stable

cli_commands.whisky new <name-or-path>: Create a new Whisky game project and run go mod tidy inside it
cli_commands.whisky run [dir]: Run a Whisky project using the entry_point declared in whisky.json
cli_commands.whisky doctor: Inspect Go, git, pkg-config, Vulkan, X11, and Wayland availability
